arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-objdump objdump 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

data.reasoningText:
I need to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key library is `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

The frames to symbolicate are:
- #01 pc 0x003a81f0 in libcoreclr.so
- #02 pc 0x003a9244 in libcoreclr.so
- #03 pc 0x00298b10 in libcoreclr.so


- #04 pc 0x00298c48 in libcoreclr.so
- #05 pc 0x0018e3a4 in libcoreclr.so
- #06 pc 0x0018df6c in libcoreclr.so

To get the symbols for this specific build, I'll need to locate the debug symbol packages that Microsoft publishes for the .NET runtime, likely through NuGet or their symbol servers.

I'm working through the proper URL format for Microsoft's symbol server with ELF files. The build ID `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801` should be formatted as lowercase hex without dashes when querying the server, and the path structure for Linux native libraries follows a specific convention.

Rather than continuing to guess at the exact URL format, I could use the `dotnet-symbol` tool to handle the download automatically, or fall back to `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` if I have the debug symbols available locally. Let me explore what tools I actually have access to first.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-objdump objdump 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server (multiple URL patterns)
echo "=== Trying _.debug with elf-buildid-sym ==="
curl -sS -L -o /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -3

echo "=== Trying libcoreclr.so with elf-buildid ==="
curl -sS -L -o /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" \
  -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -3

echo "=== Trying libcoreclr.so.dbg ==="
curl -sS -L -o /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg" \
  -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -3
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
I have `addr2line` available. Now I need to download the debug symbols for libcoreclr.so. Let me try downloading from Microsoft's symbol server.

The BuildId is: `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`

Let me try multiple URL patterns for the Microsoft symbol server:

1. `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-<buildid>/libcoreclr.so` - for the binary itself


2. `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/_.debug` - for the debug symbols

I'll attempt both approaches to see which one works.

  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg" \
  -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -3", "description": "Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server", "initial_wait": 30}
